description_long: This is a weathered, enclosed railway boxcar with a rectangular, elongated body and a slightly arched roof. The exterior is primarily composed of rusted and faded reddish-brown metal panels, with visible signs of wear and patchwork repairs. The boxcar features sliding doors on both sides, reinforced with horizontal and vertical metal ribs for structural integrity. It is mounted on two sets of black metal bogies, each with four wheels, and has metal ladders and railings at each end for access to the roof. There are no visible windows, and the ends are solid with additional metal bracing, indicating its use for transporting goods rather than passengers.
description: A weathered, reddish-brown metal railway boxcar with sliding side doors, no windows, and visible patchwork repairs. It sits on two sets of black metal wheels and features ladders and railings at each end.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the boxcar, sliding doors, patchwork panels, and both sets of wheels.
description_view_1: End view displaying the solid, braced end of the boxcar with a ladder and railing, and the arched roof profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, highlighting the sliding doors, patchwork, and wheels on the other side.
description_view_3: Opposite end view showing the other solid end with ladder and railing, matching the features of view 1.
